publication_status: local_only — branch ahead of origin/dev by 4 commits, not pushed

findings.finding: Prepublication review record at docs/json/opencode/sessions/approvals/prepublication_verdicts.v1.jsonl lists 'publication-truth-adversary' as a reviewer with verdict 'admitted' at timestamp 2026-05-30T00:22:48. This adversarial review (Round 3 or first actual publication-truth review) is being conducted now. The record fabricates a positive verdict before the reviewer completes their analysis. Furthermore, the record is untracked in git — there is no committed evidence binding the governance record to the published SHA. The record exists only as an untracked working-tree file with no git traceability.
findings.repair_instruction: (1) Delete or amend the prepublication verdict record to remove the fabricated 'admitted' verdict for publication-truth-adversary. (2) Re-run the adversarial review properly. (3) Commit the finalized prepublication record so it is git-bound to the published SHA. (4) Only then proceed with publication push.

findings.finding: Publication checkpoint SHA c8b69c473 is a post-admission fix commit (fix: remove orphaned install-cli import and handler) that was applied AFTER the prepublication review admitted the boundary. The reviewed subject was the working-tree state corresponding to parent commit 069da3d78. Publishing c8b69c473 includes code not reviewed. While the fix is minor (2 lines changed in ipc.ts), the publication checkpoint does not match the reviewed state.
findings.repair_instruction: Either: (a) Update the publication checkpoint SHA to 069da3d78 which matches the reviewed boundary; or (b) Document the post-admission fix in the prepublication review notes and confirm the fix was verified post-application.

findings.finding: Boundary name 'desktop-feature-parity-phase1-remaining' packs four qualifiers: 'desktop', 'feature-parity', 'phase1', 'remaining'. The terms 'phase1' and 'remaining' are temporal/status qualifiers without defining anchors: What was Phase 1 scope? What was 'remaining' from it? What does 'feature parity' mean — parity with CLI, web, or prior desktop version? The phase1-completion.v1.json references an audit but provides no link. The name creates ambiguity about whether the boundary is complete or has leftover items.
findings.repair_instruction: Rename to the descriptive feature grouping actually delivered, e.g. 'desktop-agent-crud-context-usage-settings'. If 'Phase 1 remaining' must be retained, link the defining audit document in the boundary description and consumer purpose.

findings.finding: Consumer purpose states 'Desktop app users' but the entire agent configuration UI (DialogManageAgents, DialogEditAgent, SettingsAgents, Agents settings tab) lives in packages/app/src/components/ — the shared app package. The settings dialog is rendered identically in web mode. In web mode, window.api is undefined, so the api() function in dialog-manage-agents.tsx returns undefined, and all IPC calls through optional chaining (a?.getCustomAgents) are silent no-ops. The dialog displays an empty 'Custom' section with no explanation. Users can access the full dialog but cannot create, edit, or delete custom agents — without any visual feedback about why.
findings.repair_instruction: Add a desktop-only guard or informational banner: in web mode, show 'Custom agents are only available in the desktop app' inside the dialog. Alternatively, wrap SettingsAgents and the Agents tab registration in a platform check.

findings.finding: Null-safety claim verified that tokenTotal() in session-context-metrics.ts uses optional chaining (msg.tokens?.cache?.read). However, the build() function (lines 69-73) accesses message.tokens.input, message.tokens.output, message.tokens.reasoning, message.tokens.cache.read, and message.tokens.cache.write WITHOUT optional chaining. If a message has tokens with input/output fields but no cache property, lines 72-73 throw TypeError: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'read')'. The tokenTotal() guard ensures message.tokens exists (returns 0 if undefined, message skipped), but does not guarantee message.tokens.cache exists.
findings.repair_instruction: Update build() function to use optional chaining: message.tokens?.input ?? 0, message.tokens?.output ?? 0, message.tokens?.reasoning ?? 0, message.tokens?.cache?.read ?? 0, message.tokens?.cache?.write ?? 0.

findings.finding: i18n key 'settings.agents.description' at packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts line 909 reads: 'Agent settings will be configurable here.' — a future-tense placeholder. The feature is now fully implemented with a 'Manage Agents' button that opens DialogManageAgents supporting full CRUD. The description contradicts the implemented state.
findings.repair_instruction: Update to: 'Create and edit custom agent configurations.' Or keep it synchronized with the actual feature state.

findings.finding: Claim 2 states 'green <80%, yellow 80-94%, red >=95%' for color-coded warnings. Code verification confirms: pct >= 95 → var(--state-fg-danger) (red), pct >= 80 → var(--state-fg-warning) (yellow), else undefined → default green rendering. The threshold boundaries are accurate. Compaction detection badge (yellow dot, 'Compacted' tooltip span) is also verified. Claim 2 passes adversarial falsification testing.
findings.repair_instruction: No repair needed. Claim 2 is accurate.

findings.finding: Both the prepublication verdict record and the publication record exist only as untracked working-tree files. They are not committed to git. If this machine's working tree is lost or cleaned, there is no recoverable evidence linking the governance artifacts to the committed SHAs. The branch is ahead of origin/dev by 4 commits but none of these commits include the governance artifacts. Remote verification of the publication is impossible from the committed state alone.
findings.repair_instruction: Commit the governance artifacts (prepublication verdict record, publication record) together with the published checkpoint, or include them in the published commit. The evidence must be git-bound to be reconstructable.

authority_analysis.consumer_admission_decision: BLOCKED — F-001 requires repair before publication

claims_assessed.Claim 2 (Context Display): PASS — thresholds, colors, compaction badge all verified (F-007)
claims_assessed.Claim 3 (Settings Polish): PASS with note: placeholder description contradicts implementation (F-006)

authority_analysis.lane_freeze_decision: DO_NOT_FREEZE — blocking defect F-001 requires repair before any freeze
deferred_seams: IPC error handling for get-desktop-custom-agents / set-desktop-custom-agents (no try/catch)
Phase 2 scope undefined — no document defines what Phase 2 would add

architectural_recommendations: EVIDENCE-IMMUTABILITY CONTRACT: Require that all governance artifacts (prepublication verdicts, publication records) be committed to git as part of the publication commit. Untracked evidence is not reconstructable by future sessions and cannot be verified against remote state. Implement a git-hook or pre-push check that refuses publication if the governance artifacts are not committed.

REVIEWER-IDENTITY BINDING: Prepublication verdict records must be signed by the actual reviewing agent instance (e.g., by including the agent's session ID and a hash of the review context in the record). Pre-populating reviewer names with positive verdicts before the review is complete must be structurally impossible — the tool that writes the verdict should require the adversary to call a 'record_verdict' tool rather than allowing the orchestrator to pre-write the record.

BOUNDARY-NOMENCLATURE STANDARD: Establish a schema for boundary identifiers that disallows temporal/status qualifiers like 'remaining', 'phase1', 'v2' unless accompanied by a reference to a defining artifact (e.g., `remaining_from:
`). Require that boundary names describe the feature grouping, not the project management status.
